projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The network consistently reports 15-19 connected devices, far exceeding the household's expected count, which combined with no camera activity at 9:57 PM strongly suggests an unlogged gathering, visitors, or a large number of active IoT devices. This warrants a quick check to ensure network stability and confirm who is on the premises.

The network consistently reports 14-16 connected mobile devices, which is significantly higher than expected for your household on a Wednesday evening. This suggests either unexpected guests are present or a network device-counting anomaly is occurring.

The device count stabilizing at 11-14 this Wednesday morning aligns with the family departing for work and school, but the persistent count likely includes smart home hubs and IoT devices rather than people. This confirms the house is effectively empty and prevents false presence assumptions or unnecessary check-ins.

The connected device count dropped sharply from 16 to 11 between 08:32 and 08:38, indicating the family is likely departing for the day. This aligns with typical Wednesday morning schedules and suggests automations like security arming or climate adjustments could be triggered.

The highly regular oscillation between 18 and 19 devices every ~5 minutes, combined with zero camera activity and Roxanne’s confirmed low sleep, indicates a network or presence-tracking glitch (likely a bedroom device or router pinging the network) rather than actual household movement. This warrants a quick network check to ensure presence data isn’t masking real events or causing false alerts.
Roxanne slept only 2.89h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.

The stable 14-17 device count with zero camera motion on a weekday evening strongly indicates normal family downtime in bedrooms or camera blind spots, confirming the pattern is expected and requires no action.

The sustained 13–18 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours strongly suggests a network presence detection anomaly or unregistered devices rather than actual occupancy, which could mask real activity or indicate a Wi-Fi handshake issue.

The sustained 14–18 device count paired with zero camera motion for four hours suggests either unregistered guests/devices are occupying camera-blind zones or a network counting anomaly is inflating the total; the recent spike to 18 devices warrants verifying if the family has returned or if IoT devices are being miscounted as mobile endpoints.

The sustained 13–16 device connections paired with zero camera motion on a weekday afternoon strongly suggests the house is unoccupied or quiet, with devices remaining idle in the background; a quick verification of the family’s schedule or off-camera activity is recommended.
